description_long: This is a compact, single-story bungalow with a green exterior and a prominent, wide-hipped roof covered in gray shingles. The front facade features a small covered porch with a gabled roof above the entrance, flanked by two symmetrically placed windows. The sides and rear of the house display additional windows, maintaining a balanced and orderly appearance. The structure sits slightly elevated above the ground, with a small set of steps leading up to the front door. The overall design is simple and functional, with clean lines and minimal ornamentation. The house is typical of early to mid-20th-century American residential architecture, suitable for suburban or urban neighborhoods.
description: A green, single-story bungalow with a wide gray-hipped roof, front porch, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_0: Front view showing the gabled porch, central door, and two symmetrically placed windows.
description_view_1: Side view with three evenly spaced windows and the hip roof extending over the structure.
description_view_2: Rear-side view showing two windows and the extension of the hip roof, with a small rear bump-out.
description_view_3: Back view with a central door and two windows, plus a visible rear extension under the roof.
